channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Centrist former Representative Melissa Bean wins Illinois’ 8th District Democratic primary
item.description: Former U.S. Representative Melissa Bean has won the Democratic primary for Illinois’ 8th Congressional District, marking a political comeback after losing her seat in 2010 during the Tea Party wave. B...

item.title: Escalation and Diplomacy in the U.S.–Israel–Iran Conflict of 2026
item.description: The Wall Street Journal’s live coverage tracks an intensifying confrontation between the United States, Israel, and Iran in early 2026, marked by cross‑border strikes, missile attacks, and failed or a...

item.title: Pakistan, Turkiye and Egypt help secure pause in US plans to strike Iranian energy sites
item.description: On the 24th day of the US‑Israel war against Iran, Pakistan has moved to the centre of a diplomatic effort, working with Turkiye and Egypt to secure at least a five‑day pause in US plans to strike Ira...

item.title: Sindh government reviews slow progress in reconstruction of over 19,000 flood-damaged schools
item.description: Sindh Chief Minister Syed Murad Ali Shah was briefed on the status of school rehabilitation efforts following the devastating 2022 floods, which damaged over 19,500 educational institutions across the...

item.title: First Episode of Saturday Night Live UK Impresses with British Talent and Format Success
item.description: The debut episode of Saturday Night Live UK has exceeded expectations, achieving a strong viewership of 226,000 on Sky One and outperforming Channel 4 in the same timeslot. Skepticism about the compat...

item.title: Rising fuel prices linked to Iran conflict may drive higher food costs in British Columbia
item.description: Fuel prices in British Columbia remain elevated as geopolitical tensions involving Iran continue to affect global oil markets, with gas prices in the Lower Mainland hovering around $2.14 per litre. Ex...

item.title: Pentagon Official Emil Michael Reflects on Uber Exit and Ongoing Dispute with Anthropic
item.description: Emil Michael, currently serving as a senior technology official at the U.S. Department of Defense, has resurfaced in the headlines amid the Pentagon’s clash with AI company Anthropic. In a recent podc...

item.title: Poor Vision Among Drivers Heightens Risk of Road Accidents in Kenya
item.description: On Kenya's highways, especially the busy Nairobi-Mombasa A109 Road, uncorrected poor vision among drivers is emerging as a serious but often overlooked risk factor. Many motorists, including delivery ...

item.title: Reburial of 63 Khoi and San ancestors marks restoration of dignity and cultural healing
item.description: The recent reburial of 63 Khoi and San ancestors at the Kinderlê Museum in Steinkopf, Northern Cape, represents a significant act of dignity restoration and cultural healing for the Khoisan community....

item.title: Soludo Announces Key Government Appointments to Strengthen Anambra Administration
item.description: Governor Chukwuma Soludo don approve fresh appointments of key officials and special advisers to serve for different positions inside Anambra State Government, as part of effort to make governance str...

item.title: Colombian air force C‑130 Hercules crashes after takeoff in Amazon region, at least 8 dead, 71 rescued
item.description: BOGOTA: Colombia ke air force ka Hercules C‑130 jahaz, jis mein kehlaya ja raha hai ke 125 log sawaar thay, sahib‑e‑hasrat 8 logon ke sath Amazon ke jangalay choonon mein crash ho gaya. Muqammi aqarar...

item.title: Government begins ₱5,000 cash aid distribution to TNVS drivers in Metro Manila amid fuel price hikes
item.description: Nagsimula na ang pamamahagi ng cash assistance para sa mga Transport Network Vehicle Service (TNVS) drivers sa Metro Manila bilang tugon sa patuloy na pagtaas ng presyo ng langis. Ayon sa Department o...

item.title: Los Angeles Jury Orders Bill Cosby to Pay $59.25 Million in 1972 Sexual Assault Civil Case
item.description: A civil jury in Los Angeles has found comedian and actor Bill Cosby liable for a 1972 sexual assault against Donna Motsinger, now 84 years old. The jury awarded Motsinger a total of $59.25 million in ...

item.title: Examining Petron’s Privatization and the Ownership of Strategic Land Assets
item.description: Petron Corporation’s privatization in 1994 has raised long-term economic concerns, particularly over ownership of the land under its refineries and depots. Initially, the government, through the Phili...

item.title: Proposed labour law amendments could extend full employment protections to South Africa’s platform workers
item.description: South Africa’s Department of Employment and Labour has proposed wide-ranging amendments to key labour laws, including the Basic Conditions of Employment Act, the Employment Equity Act and the National...

item.title: Victor Osimhen Faces Six-Week Recovery After Successful Arm Surgery
item.description: Nigerian striker Victor Osimhen has successfully gone through surgery after he suffer fracture for him right forearm during Galatasaray match against Liverpool for UEFA Champions League Round of 16. T...

item.title: Pauline Hanson's rising support linked to influential connections
item.description: Pauline Hanson, leader of the One Nation party, is experiencing a notable surge in voter support across Australia. Analysts suggest this increase is less about her political platform and more about th...

item.title: US Policy Threatens Healthcare Access in Developing Countries by Targeting Cuban Medical Missions
item.description: A concerning trend is emerging as the United States intensifies its efforts to undermine Cuba's international medical programmes. For decades, Cuba has provided vital healthcare services to developing...

item.title: India and Nepal: Evolving Security Dynamics in a Changing Regional Order
item.description: The article by former CRPF Director General A.P. Maheshwari analyses how the traditional warmth between India and Nepal has transformed amid changing political, economic, and security realities. Histo...

item.title: President Samia Emphasizes Religious Freedom and National Unity
item.description: Dar es Salaam — President Samia Suluhu Hassan has stressed the importance of protecting religious freedom in Tanzania while maintaining national unity and peace. Speaking during the Eid Baraza as Musl...
